biography.education: Skidmore College, B.A. French & International Affairs, 1999 (Honors)
biography.notableHeritage: Granddaughter of Muhsin al-Barazi, former Prime Minister of Syria
biography.paragraph1: Born in Boston to an American father and a Syrian mother, Arwa Damon spent her early years in Wayland, Massachusetts before her family moved to Morocco and then to Istanbul, Turkey — where her father served as a teacher and director at Robert College. She skipped sixth grade and graduated with honors at 16, later earning her degree from Skidmore College.
biography.paragraph2: It was the attacks of September 11, 2001 that set Damon on the path of journalism. She moved to Baghdad before the start of the Iraq War, beginning her career at CameraPlanet before transitioning to CNN as a freelance contributor in 2004 and becoming a full correspondent in 2006. Over the following sixteen years, she reported from some of the planet's most dangerous and heartbreaking places.
biography.paragraph3: In June 2022, Damon left CNN to devote herself entirely to INARA, the humanitarian organization she co-founded in Beirut in 2015 — continuing her life's work of standing beside the world's most vulnerable populations.

timeline.title: The Call to Report
timeline.description: Inspired by the September 11 attacks, Damon abandoned a career in textiles and moved to Baghdad ahead of the Iraq War. She joined CameraPlanet as a media supplier before embedding with CNN's Baghdad bureau as a freelancer in 2004.

timeline.title: Chronicling Iraq
timeline.description: Damon covered all three Iraqi elections — January 2005, October's constitutional referendum, and December 2005 — as well as the historic trials and executions of Saddam Hussein and his senior officials in January 2007. She joined CNN as a full correspondent in 2006.

timeline.title: Arab Spring & Benghazi
timeline.description: As part of the CNN team that won a 2012 Emmy Award for outstanding coverage of the Egyptian Revolution, Damon documented the pivotal fall of President Mubarak. Following the 2012 Benghazi attack, she was among the first journalists on the scene and recovered the personal diary of slain Ambassador J. Christopher Stevens.

timeline.title: Investigations Across Continents
timeline.description: Damon embedded with anti-poaching rangers in the Congo's Odzala National Park for her investigation Ivory War, then travelled to West Africa following the Chibok schoolgirls kidnapping. She covered the Syrian civil war extensively, including multiple reporting trips into refugee camps.

timeline.title: Trapped in the Battle of Mosul
timeline.description: In one of her most harrowing assignments, Damon was embedded with Iraqi forces during the Battle of Mosul. Her convoy came under sustained IS fire, leaving her trapped for 28 hours in intense fighting before Iraqi reinforcements broke through to rescue them.

timeline.title: Environment & Exploration
timeline.description: Damon accompanied Greenpeace expeditions to Antarctica and the Arctic, reporting on catastrophic ice loss and the global climate crisis. She also reported from Nepal on the surge in fatalities among Everest climbers and helped keep the Thai cave rescue in the global spotlight.

timeline.title: Full-Time Humanitarian Work
timeline.description: After departing CNN in June 2022, Damon focused entirely on INARA. She has entered the Gaza Strip four times to lead charity work during the ongoing conflict, continuing to place herself at the center of the world's most urgent humanitarian crises.

inara.missionLine: A world where every child has the agency to create a dignified life.
inara.paragraph1: The International Network for Aid, Relief and Assistance (INARA) is a U.S.-registered 501(c)(3) humanitarian organization founded in 2015 by Arwa Damon. Established in response to critical gaps in the humanitarian system, INARA works to support children affected by conflict, displacement, and natural and human-induced disasters who are often unable to access sustained medical and psychosocial care.
inara.paragraph2: INARA delivers integrated, child-centered programming that combines life-saving interventions with long-term recovery support. The organization focuses on children facing complex and overlapping vulnerabilities — including injury, trauma, disability, and exclusion from essential services. Through a multidisciplinary approach, INARA provides access to medical treatment, mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS), and coordinated case management, ensuring continuity of care beyond the immediate emergency phase.
inara.paragraph3: Its interventions span comprehensive medical and rehabilitative care; trauma-informed community-based MHPSS services; livelihood and economic empowerment to strengthen household stability; education in emergencies; WASH interventions; and a rapid emergency response capacity for immediate medical, psychosocial, food, and non-food assistance during acute crises.

vmv.vision: A world where every child has the agency to create a dignified life.
vmv.mission: Fostering a future where we empower children to shape their own dignified lives.
vmv.purpose: Enhancing children's capacity to shape their own fulfilling futures through integrated health, empowerment, and community-driven interventions.

pillars.title: Medical & Rehabilitative Care
pillars.description: Specialized treatment, long-term follow-up, and comprehensive rehabilitative care for injured and disabled children.

pillars.title: Mental Health & MHPSS
pillars.description: Trauma-informed, community-based mental health and psychosocial support services for children and families.

pillars.title: Education in Emergencies
pillars.description: Safe, inclusive learning environments and livelihood empowerment to restore stability and future prospects.

pillars.title: Rapid Emergency Response
pillars.description: Immediate deployment of medical, psychosocial, food, and non-food assistance during acute crises.

stats.label: Of donations go to children in need

inara.countriesSubtitle: INARA currently operates across six countries, delivering holistic care in some of the world's most challenging environments.

articles.title: Iran’s Afghan Refugees Flee Homeward
articles.excerpt: Thousands are returning daily at the Islam Qala border crossing as Afghans trade precarious life in Iran for an uncertain future in Afghanistan.

articles.title: Bringing Color Back to Syria
articles.excerpt: A reflection on wartime trauma among Syria’s children and the long road toward healing.

articles.title: The Devastation of Lebanon
articles.excerpt: Why many Lebanese view the latest strikes not as a war on Hezbollah, but as a war on everyone.

articles.title: Gaza in a Million Pieces
articles.excerpt: After six months, Damon is allowed into Gaza and finds a war being waged mainly against children.

articles.title: Former CNN reporter Arwa Damon on the war in Ukraine one year later and the need for aid in Turkey and Syria
articles.excerpt: Damon discusses the war in Ukraine, disaster response after the Turkey-Syria earthquake, and why she founded INARA.

articles.title: Gaza under siege: A doctor recounts the humanitarian cost of war
articles.excerpt: A firsthand account from surgeon Ghassan Abu Sitta of Gaza’s collapsing healthcare system under siege.

articles.title: I covered the battle against ISIS in Mosul. Gaza’s challenges will make it look like child’s play.
articles.excerpt: Damon draws hard-earned lessons from Mosul to explain why Gaza’s battlefield realities and humanitarian stakes are even more severe.

articles.title: I spent two decades as a CNN correspondent. We journalists need to do a better job reporting the truth.
articles.excerpt: An argument for journalism that confronts propaganda, dehumanization, and polarization—especially in wartime.

articles.title: Journalist Arwa Damon on Conflict, Humanity and the Stories That Stay With You
articles.excerpt: Arwa Damon reflects on two decades of war reporting, the psychological cost of bearing witness, and why she ultimately chose humanitarian work over the camera.

articles.title: On the Ground in Gaza — With Arwa Damon
articles.excerpt: Damon discusses her four entries into the Gaza Strip to lead INARA's humanitarian response, and the scenes she encountered that even decades of war reporting could not prepare her for.

articles.title: With Israel's Expanding War, My Colleagues in Gaza Fear a 'Death Blow'
articles.excerpt: A first-person account of the deteriorating humanitarian conditions in Gaza and the threat posed by plans to restructure aid delivery.

articles.title: Arctic Melt: Threat Beneath the Ice
articles.excerpt: Reporting from a Greenpeace expedition to the Arctic, Damon documents the alarming pace of polar ice loss and its consequences for global ecosystems.

articles.title: Palestinians Feel They Are Being 'Slowly Exterminated'
articles.excerpt: Damon speaks directly about the healthcare crisis inside Gaza and the moral weight of witnessing systematic destruction of civilian infrastructure.

articles.title: The arduous system for getting aid into Gaza
articles.excerpt: Arwa Damon’s charity — International Network for Aid, Relief and Assistance — has tried to send aid to Gaza, facing rejections and obstacles. The episode discusses the economics of running a humanitarian nonprofit and barriers to aid delivery.

articles.title: How generational trauma among Israelis and Palestinians fuels the cycles of violence
articles.excerpt: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Arwa Damon, former international foreign correspondent for CNN, about the impact of trauma on Israelis and Palestinians as a result of successive eruptions of violence.

articles.title: A 'Devastating Scale' Of Suffering In Syria
articles.excerpt: NPR's Lulu Garcia-Navarro speaks to CNN's Arwa Damon about the unfolding humanitarian crisis in Idlib as Turkish and Russian-backed Syrian government forces fight for control of the territory.

articles.title: For Days, Turkey Has Been Targeting Kurds In Northern Syria
articles.excerpt: As Turkey continues its attack against Kurdish towns in northern Syria, NPR's Lulu Garcia-Navarro speaks to CNN correspondent Arwa Damon, who is reporting near Turkey's border with Syria.

articles.title: CNN Defends Reporting On Slain Ambassador's Diary
articles.excerpt: CNN reporter Arwa Damon found Ambassador Stevens' personal journal in Benghazi, revealing his fears about security threats. The report sparked debate about journalistic ethics.

articles.title: A Passion To Bear Witness: Why War Correspondents Take The Risk
articles.excerpt: CNN's Arwa Damon made it into Homs, Syria, under siege, to report on the conflict. The article explores the motivations and risks for war correspondents.

articles.title: Trial Looms For Sole Defendant In 2012 Benghazi Attack That Killed Ambassador
articles.excerpt: Coverage of the Benghazi attack, with reporting from Arwa Damon on the ground, as the trial for the accused begins in Washington, D.C.

articles.title: INARA’s Mobile Clinic in Syria
articles.excerpt: INARA’s mobile clinic, led by Arwa Damon’s organization, reaches vulnerable communities in Syria, providing medical care in remote areas.

articles.title: A Humanitarian crisis on the Afghan-Iranian borders
articles.excerpt: INARA, founded by Arwa Damon, assesses urgent needs at the Afghan-Iranian border amid a rapidly escalating humanitarian crisis.

articles.title: Food parcels for displaced Gazan families in Egypt
articles.excerpt: INARA distributes food parcels to Gazan families displaced to Egypt, addressing food insecurity and financial hardship.

articles.title: As Gaza Braces for a Ground Invasion

articles.title: One Year After Oct. 7

articles.title: Syria + Trump Oval Office Meeting
